all.title: Marcos Aerospace Unveils the Quencher concept
all.description: This firefighting aircraft promises to do it all – amphibious, pilotless, day and night ops, fleet coordination and an AI brain. Marcos Aerospace, a Greek aerospace startup based in Athens, has just launched Quencher, an autonomous amphibious aircraft currently in what the company is describing as “the preliminary design phase.” With a 4,500-litre water capacity, […]

all.title: Elevated fire weather forecasted throughout the US this week
all.description: The “very active” fire season predicted for the U.S. since the beginning of the year may largely kick off this week, according to forecasts from the National Interagency Fire Center. Elevated fire weather conditions are set across the Southwest and southern Great Basin regions due to strong winds, dry conditions and very warm temperatures, according […]

all.title: US Wildland Fire Service partially funded from significant EPA cuts
all.description: It’s been nearly half a year since the U.S. Wildland Fire Service was formally launched, but the new agency has gone without funding since. Lawmakers may soon change that. The U.S. House Committee on Appropriations submitted its funding plan for the Department of the Interior, the environment and related agencies this week, with a specific […]

all.title: Europe preps for a wild wildfire summer
all.description: Europe has boosted its wildfire preparations ahead of a forecasted busy 2026 season by deploying its largest force yet of firefighters, aircraft and other experts across the continent. The announcement comes as the World Meteorological Organisation issued a Global Seasonal Climate Update that, while highlighting the development of a strong El Nino around the Pacific, […]

all.title: IAWF at Wildfire Camp in Germany
all.description: We are at Interschutz 2026 in Hanover, Germany all this week: 1-6 June 2026. IAWF Vice President Ciaran Nugent is hosting a small display outdoors in the Wildfire Camp with a small group of colleagues. For the first time in its 70-year history the large Interschutz fire and rescue services trade fair in Germany features […]

all.title: Global wildfire report for 2025 notes drop in area burned, as USAID cuts threaten future reports
all.description: Wildfires filled the headlines of 2025 from the US to South Korea to Spain and beyond, but an annual report shows that last year was actually one of the lowest fire years of this century. At the global scale, land area burnt by wildfires in 2025 was nearly 828 million acres, which was the second-lowest […]

all.title: Save the environment, and the economy, with prescribed burning, researchers say
all.description: The environmental benefits of fighting bad fire with good fire have been documented for decades, but researchers are now working to show just how much economic good prescribed burning brings. A study recently published in the journal Science integrated and analyzed U.S. Forest Service data from 285 wildfires that intersected with prescribed burning and other […]

all.title: Power agency that destroyed Pacific Northwest wildfire evidence ‘completely rewrites’ mitigation plan for 2026
all.description: The Pacific Northwest’s federal power agency, recently found guilty of destroying evidence that may have tied it to a disastrous 2020 wildfire, has released its latest fire mitigation plan. The Bonneville Power Administration released this year’s updated plan on Wednesday, with officials saying the plan had been “completely rewritten” to elevate the agency’s risk mode

all.title: Water pollution lasts for years after a wildfire burns, new research shows
all.description: Contaminants from wildfires can create long term water quality concerns in areas far from the fire-impacted zones as drifting smoke plumes and rainfall runoff challenge standard water filtration techniques. all.title: Wildfire science editing award
all.description: Congratulations to Dr Xinyan Huang, recipient of the International Journal of Wildland Fire Outstanding Associate Editor Award for 2025. This award honours Associate Editors who have demonstrated sustained excellence in their responsiveness, thoroughness, and timeliness. Dr Huang has served as an Associate Editor of the International Journal of Wildland Fire (IJWF) since 2021. He is […]

all.title: Ontario murder suspect believed to be in rural B.C.: police
all.description: A suspect wanted for second-degree murder in the death of a man in southern Ontario may be camping somewhere in rural British Columbia.
